summary:
This week's news briefing covers March 23-29, 2026, highlighting persistent conflicts in the Middle East and Africa, alongside developments in Ukraine and other global events.

The most significant story was escalating tensions between Iran and Israel, which dominated the week. On March 24, Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel and Gulf Arab states amid Trump peace talks. Israel responded with fresh strikes across Iran on March 26 as ceasefire talks stalled. Iran's Revolutionary Guards followed with strikes on Israel and Gulf sites on March 27, injuring 12 U.S. troops at Saudi Prince Sultan Air Base on March 28. Houthi rebels launched their first missile at Israel from Yemen that day. On March 29, Iranian Guards struck aluminum plants in Bahrain and UAE. Trump extended Iran's deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to April 6 on March 27. This multi-day escalation proved the week's most lasting conflict.

In Africa, Sudanese army drone strikes hit a hospital in East Darfur on March 23, killing 64 and wounding 89; the story persisted into March 25 coverage. Rwanda-backed rebels overran Congolese troops and entered Goma on March 23, 27, and 28, displacing one million by March 27, marking a developing crisis despite some one-day reports fading.

Ukraine saw Russian forces intensify attacks in the south on March 26 after Kyiv gains momentum. The U.S. tied security guarantees to Ukraine ceding Russian-occupied Donbas, prompting a warning from Zelensky.

Other notable events included a New Mexico jury fining Meta $375 million on March 25 for child safety violations, persisting in discussions. The UN General Assembly voted 123-3-52 on March 26 to designate the African slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.

Aviation tragedies struck on March 24: a Colombian military plane crash killed 66 of 128 onboard; an Air Canada jet collided with a firetruck at LaGuardia Airport, killing the pilots.

Stories that faded quickly: IEA head's March 23 warning of Iran war's economic threat; initial Rwanda-Goma reports; WMO's ocean heat records; NASA-SpaceX Crew-10 launch; NATO's March 25 response to Baltic Sea cable sabotage.

North Korea's Kim Jong-un oversaw a 2,500-kilonewton ICBM engine test on March 29. An Israeli strike killed three journalists in southern Lebanon that day.

No major resolutions occurred; Middle East and African conflicts persisted without de-escalation. (378 words)
